title: hieracium cerinthoides subsp. rhomboidale
intro: Hieracium cerinthoides subsp. rhomboidale, also known as Hawkweed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and support immune function. Hawkweed can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
